REST API accessible from any language

scaling_models: Horizontal auto-scaling by adding TiDB/TiKV/TiFlash nodes, automatic data rebalancing
Horizontal scaling supported through containerization, cloud deployment on AWS, Heroku, DigitalOcean
Horizontal scaling supported through stateless design with shared database and Redis backend
Horizontal scaling through WordPress multisite, vertical scaling through server resources
Horizontal scaling through distributed clusters with automatic sharding and replication, vertical scaling through multi-core vectorized processing
Horizontal scaling through multiple container instances with load balancer, database connection pooling for performance
Horizontal scaling through multiple worker processes, database replication support
Horizontal scaling via multiple Rails instances, separate streaming service, Redis cluster support, CDN for media assets
Horizontal scaling via multiple application servers behind load balancer, separate Tornado processes for WebSocket handling
Horizontal scaling via multiple web server and Celery worker containers, Redis clustering for cache, database read replicas
Horizontal scaling with Infinispan clustering for session replication and load balancing
Horizontal scaling with load balancer, Celery workers can scale independently, database sharding support
Horizontal scaling with microservices architecture and message queuing
Single instance deployment with database as bottleneck. Horizontal scaling possible with shared database and storage.
Single-instance application, no horizontal scaling (game engine)
Single-instance architecture designed for home/small business use, horizontal scaling not supported
Single-process desktop application with plugin-based feature scaling
Single-user desktop application with optional API server for remote access
Single-user desktop application, multi-user via Ghidra Server for collaborative analysis
Single-user desktop application, no scaling considerations
Unicorn HTTP server supports process-based scaling, PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch can be scaled independently
horizontal scaling through load balancing, vertical scaling for database, Redis clustering for cache distribution
horizontal scaling through multi-site architecture, load balancing via web server
